testcase.name: @stynx/data barrel > exports at least one symbol
createStynxPgClient > constructs a pg.Client from a connection string
createStynxPgClient > passes through host/port/user/database options
Database > rejects reader transactions that are not readonly
Database > rejects app transactions without tenant context
Database > rejects owner transactions outside system context
Database > rejects app transactions without actor context when tenant context exists
Database > applies request session state, readonly mode, deadline, commit, context cleanup, and release
Database > maps PostgreSQL readonly and statement-timeout errors during rollback
Database > preserves the original transaction error when rollback also fails
Database > does not retry non-PostgreSQL errors or retryable codes when retry is disabled
Database > retries retryable errors and reports the final serialization failure context
Database > describes branch: treats a non-finite retry count as immediate serialization failure
Database > waits the computed jitter before retrying retryable transaction failures
Database > does not classify non-Error retry codes or null transaction errors as retryable
Database > does not clear a CLS transaction slot that is already inactive in finally
Database > rejects replica write transactions before resolving tenant context
Database > uses replica pool options through withReplica
Database > delegates system context without changing the provided execution context
Database > runs nested transactions with savepoints and maps nested errors
Database > resolves owner execution context from the system context
Database > omits optional owner request and actor GUCs when system context lacks them
data errors > exposes stable error codes and context
data errors > omits optional error context when no context is provided
StynxMigrationRunner.onModuleInit > no-ops when migrations option is missing
StynxMigrationRunner.onModuleInit > no-ops when migrations.enabled is false
StynxMigrationRunner.onModuleInit > runs platform migrations when migrations.enabled is true
StynxMigrationRunner.onModuleInit > invokes the adopter-supplied runner after platform migrations
StynxMigrationRunner.runPlatformMigrations (mocked client) > initializes pools + creates schema_migrations table + releases client
StynxMigrationRunner.runPlatformMigrations (mocked client) > skips migrations whose id is already in core.schema_migrations
StynxMigrationRunner.runPlatformMigrations (mocked client) > rolls back when a migration body throws and rethrows the error
StynxMigrationRunner.runPlatformMigrations (mocked client) > continues migration-dir probing and preserves migration errors when rollback fails
StynxMigrationRunner.runPlatformMigrations (mocked client) > falls back to the packaged migration path when every probe misses
createStynxPgPool > creates a Pool from a connection string
createStynxPgPool > swallows idle pool errors from the factory-created pool
createStynxPgPool > passes ssl: true through as { rejectUnauthorized: false }
createStynxPgPool > honors host/port/user/password/database/max/applicationName options
StynxPoolRegistry > creates owner + app + reader pools on init
StynxPoolRegistry > is idempotent — re-init does not re-create pools
StynxPoolRegistry > get(owner) returns the owner pool by default
StynxPoolRegistry > get(app, replica=true) returns the reader pool
StynxPoolRegistry > get(reader) always returns the reader pool
StynxPoolRegistry > resolves connection strings via secretId when no connectionString provided
StynxPoolRegistry > honors JSON-shaped secrets via parseSecretConnection (connectionString field)
StynxPoolRegistry > honors JSON-shaped secrets with `url` field
StynxPoolRegistry > describes branch: applies ssl for registry-managed pools
StynxPoolRegistry > falls through to raw secret when JSON lacks a connection field
StynxPoolRegistry > falls through to raw secret when JSON parse fails
StynxPoolRegistry > throws when a connection has neither connectionString nor secretId
query helpers > builds withDeleted SQL with shifted placeholders, tenant guard, ordering, limit, and offset
query helpers > builds onlyDeleted SQL with default archive ordering and no tenant guard for non-tenant tables
query helpers > builds onlyDeleted SQL with explicit predicates, archive ordering, limit, and offset
query helpers > builds onlyDeleted predicates for non-tenant tables without a tenant guard
query helpers > builds withDeleted SQL without predicates, ordering, limits, or tenant guard
query helpers > accepts undefined where clauses and empty ordering lists
query helpers > describes branch: overrides archive ordering through the chainable orderBy helper
query helpers > maps archived rows and keeps promise helpers delegated to execute
query helpers > delegates rejected executions through then and catch callbacks
query helpers > proxies soft-deletable select builders and leaves live-only builders unchanged
withSystemContext > delegates to database.withSystemContext with the same reason + fn
table metadata helpers > quotes identifiers and builds qualified relation names
table metadata helpers > defaults unschemed tables to public metadata
table metadata helpers > uses explicit schema metadata when present
Transaction > delegates active read/write primitives and rejects all primitives after close
Transaction > guards write operations for reader transactions
Transaction > rejects non-soft-deletable archive operations before querying mirrors
Transaction > forces cascade restore options through restoreWithCascade defaults
Transaction > softDelete returns dry-run plans and filters the root archive entry from committed cascades
Transaction > uses archive table parsing, confirmation, metrics, and archive-size labels for hard deletes
Transaction > builds recursive cascade plans with exact row counts and depth limits
Transaction > soft-deletes one reference and maps missing rows and FK delete failures
Transaction > blocks soft-delete references when cascade depth, row count, or blocking children fail
Transaction > describes branch: softDelete uses real registry queries and archive-size sampling without metrics
Transaction > restores archived references, maps restore conflicts, and cascades child restores
Transaction > describes branch: restoreFromArchive cascades archived parents through real lookup helpers
Transaction > describes blocking children with counts and samples only for non-empty block rows
Transaction > finds archived parents only when the parent is absent from live rows and present in archive
Transaction > detects restore conflicts from array and text constraint column metadata
Transaction > loads column names, checks mirrors, normalizes labels, and samples archive sizes defensively
